document.docAuthor: Yang, Lin, Long, Jian-Kun, Chang, Zhi-Min & Chen, Xiang-Sheng
document.docTitle: Machaerotypus nodulus Li & Chen 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Two new species of the treehopper genus Machaerotypus Uhler, 1896 from China (Hemiptera: Membracidae: Centrotinae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Two new species of the treehopper genus Machaerotypus Uhler, 1896 from China (Hemiptera: Membracidae: Centrotinae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China. The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China.

Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China. The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China.

Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China. The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China.

Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China. The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou, 550025, P. R. China.

subSubSection.paragraph:
The species name ‘
nodulus
’ is derived from the Latin word ‘ nodus ’ (knot), referring to the nodules along the forewing veins.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Holotype
CHINA • ♂ ; Guizhou Province , Dafang Country ; 27°19′ N, 105°61′ E;
Mar. 2019
; Xiu-dong Huang leg.; collected with yellow pan traps ; IEGU .

caption.paragraph:
Fig. 5.
Machaerotypus nodulus Li & Chen
sp. nov. , holotype, ♂ (IEGU). A–C . Habitus in frontal, lateral and dorsal view. D . Forewing. E . Hind wing. Scale bars: A–C = 1 mm; D–E = 0.5 mm.

Fig. 6.
Machaerotypus nodulus Li & Chen
sp. nov. , holotype, ♂ (IEGU). A–C . Genitalia in lateral, ventral and posterior view. D . Lateral plate. E–F . Aedeagus in posterior and lateral view. G . Style, dorsal view. Scale bars = 0.2 mm.

subSubSection.paragraph: MEASUREMENTS. Male (n = 1) body length: 4.9 mm , forewing length: 4.1 mm , width between humeral angle apices: 1.9 mm , width between suprahumeral horn apices: 1.6 mm .

COLORATION. Orange-brown marked with golden yellow except the head, ocelli, callosity, apical of humeral angle and posterior pronotal process, inner of legs black, surfaces of legs rusty red. Forewing and veins pale yellow with the following parts brown: nodulations, apical, anal angle, apical and onethird of apical, crescent-shaped stripe near basal fourth forewing.

caption.paragraph:
Fig. 7. Geographic distribution of species of
Machaerotypus
Uhler, 1896: 1
.
M. arisanus ( Kato, 1928 )
. 2 .
M. camelliae Chou & Yuan, 1981
. 3 .
M. ishiharai Kato, 1940
. 4 .
M. mali Chou & Yuan, 1981
. 5 .
M. rubronigris Funkhouser, 1938
. 6 .
M. rubromarginatus Kato, 1940
. 7 .
M. semirubronigris Yuan & Chou, 1992
. 8 .
M. sibiricus (Lethierry, 1876)
. 9 .
M. subinermis Lindberg, 1927
. 10 .
M. taibaiensis Yuan, 2002
. 11 .
M. yananensis Chou & Yuan, 1981
. 12 .
M. stigmosus Li & Chen
sp. nov. 13 .
M. nodulus Li & Chen
sp. nov.

Fig. 8. Pronotum of 13 species of genus
Machaerotypus Uhler, 1896
. A–M . Lateral view. a–g . Dorsal view. A, a .
M. camelliae Chou & Yuan, 1981
. B, b .

. C, c .

. D, d .

. E, e .

. F, f .

. G, g .

. H .
M. stigmosus Li & Chen
sp. nov. I .
M. ishiharai Kato, 1940
. J .
M. subinermis Lindberg, 1927
. K .
M. sibiricus (Lethierry, 1876)
. L .
M. arisanus ( Kato, 1928 )
. M .
M. nodulus Li & Chen
sp. nov. Figures based on Lindberg (1927) , Kato (1928) and Yuan & Chou (2002) . The dotted line represents the simulation diagram.
